title: Salmon Bay Walking Tour - Parks | seattle.gov
content: ## Visitor Center closed, no beach parking permits. **We are unable to issue beach parking permits while the Visitor Center is closed.** Parking at the beach level without a permit or ADA placard is not allowed and may result in citation or towing. Roads, paths, and bridges take you on a journey of discovery along this captivating urban wildlife corridor—the shores of Salmon Bay and beyond. Even though bulkheads, boat locks, and urban growth crowd the waterfront, your walk will take you through inspiring community projects which are underway to protect and enhance the last remnants of healthy habitat around Salmon Bay. You are invited to join your neighbors who volunteer with local groups to keep this corridor wildlife-friendly. Despite human intervention that has highly altered the estuary, Salmon Bay shelters a multitude of birds, mammals, and insects in addition to salmon and other fish. Witness the waterfront web of life here at Salmon Bay.
